The area of a container is determined by the minimum of the two heights, multiplied by the distance (width) between them.
Consider setting a pointer at the very beginning and a pointer at the very end of the array. This gives you the maximum possible width.
To potentially increase the area, you would need to increase the height. Since the area is bottlenecked by the shorter line, which pointer should you move inwards?
Always move the pointer pointing to the shorter line inward. Continue this until the two pointers meet, keeping track of the maximum area seen so far.
